key_insight: A token-based dual-view transformer can better combine CC and MLO mammography information by using dedicated fusion tokens and repeated cross-view interaction at multiple depths.
clinical_relevance: This approach may improve breast cancer image classification from routine mammograms by preserving view-specific detail while more effectively integrating complementary breast views.
method: Freeze a vision transformer backbone and adapt it with prompt-based learning.
Insert cross-attention fusion modules at multiple transformer layers.
Use dedicated fusion tokens to carry bidirectional CC-MLO information and reintegrate them into the token sequence.
evidence_signal: On VinDr-Mammo and CMMD, the method outperformed linear probing, prompt-only adaptation, and conventional fusion baselines, reaching 50.40% F1 and 0.8090 AUC on VinDr-Mammo BI-RADS classification.
watch_item: Results are promising but still depend on retrospective dataset benchmarks and should be validated on broader external cohorts and real-world clinical workflows.
paper_title: Token-Based Dual-view Fusion and Adaptation of Large Vision Models for Breast Cancer Classification
